title: Charred Crispy Moroccan Duck Couscous Royal
description: A flavorful Moroccan dish featuring charred and crispy duck confit leg, served with a side of bhuna sauce and couscous, perfect for curing hangovers, and pescatarian-friendly by substituting the traditional meat with seafood options if desired.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the duck confit leg to the skillet and sear until charred and crispy on all sides.
Remove the duck from the skillet and set aside to rest.
Add the sliced onion to the skillet and cook until softened and translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the bhuna sauce to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Bring the bhuna sauce to a simmer and let cook for 5 minutes.
Prepare the couscous according to package instructions.
Slice the rested duck confit leg into thin strips.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of couscous on a plate, top with sliced duck, and spoon the bhuna sauce over the top.
